title: Lifespan
subtitle: Why We Age — and Why We Don't Have To
author: David A. Sinclair
themes: Aging is a disease — and it can be treated
The Information Theory of Aging: aging is the loss of epigenetic information
Sirtuins, NAD+, and other longevity genes can be activated to slow aging
Aging is not inevitable — it is a process that can be slowed, halted, or reversed
Longevity is not about living forever — it is about extending healthspan, the years of healthy life
Lifestyle interventions (exercise, fasting, cold exposure) activate longevity pathways
excerpt: Aging is not a mysterious, inevitable process. It is a disease. And like any disease, it can be treated — slowed, halted, even reversed. The evidence is mounting: we are not victims of our biology. The genes that control aging are programmable. We can learn to speak their language.
